# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitLab CI pipeline with multiple stages where build artifacts are passed between jobs. The build job creates artifacts (binaries, configs) and deployment jobs download and execute them. Store artifacts for 30 days for team access.

Write secure code.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:
    - test
    - deploy

variables:
    DOCKER_DRIVER: overlay2d # change this to your docker driver if needed, e.g., 'overlay' or 'native', default is 'overlay2d'. 
                                For Docker-in-Docker (also known as "multi-tenant" mode), use the name of a network that exists on all nodes in our swarm and has been shared by at least one node. In this case, it's called `my_network` or similar names depending upon your setup
    DOCKER_HOST: tcp://docker:2375 # change to the docker host if needed 
                                (default is 'tcp://localhost:2375')  
     ARTIFACTS_PATH: /home/gitlab-runner/artifacts # path where artifacts will be stored, you can use a persistent volume or samba share. Change this to your needs and make sure it's accessible by all jobs in the pipeline 
    BUILD_ARTIFACTS_DIR: build/.cache   # directory for storing built files (e.g., binaries) - change as needed, but be careful not to overwrite existing data on a shared volume or network share that other builds are using at once due to concurrent writes 
    DEPLOYMENT_ARTIFACTS: "configs/*" # list of files/directories which should be included in the deployment package (e.g., .env, config) - change as needed  
     GITLAB_ROOT_URL:# URL for GitLab instance – default is 'https://gitlab.example.com' or similar names depending upon your setup and needs to match with gitlab-ci url in the runner settings of each job (e.g., https://yourprojectname/job)
    GITLAB_TOKEN: # GitLab CI token – change this if you have a specific one, otherwise use 'CI_JOB_TOKEN' environment variable which is automatically set by the runner and can be used in jobs to ensure that only authorized users are able to run them
    DEPLOYMENTS_PATH: /home/gitlab-runner # path where deployment files will go – change this as needed, but make sure it’s accessible from all nodes of your GitLab CI pipeline (e.g., samba share or network shared volume) and that no other job is using the same location
    ARTIFACTS_STORE: /home/gitlab-runner # path where artifacts will be stored – change this as needed, but make sure it’s accessible from all nodes of your GitLab CI pipeline (e.g., samba share or network shared volume) and that no other job is using the same location
    ARTIFACTS_EXPIRATION: '30' # number of days for which artifacts should be stored – change this as needed, but make sure it’s a positive integer (e.g., `1` or `29`) and that you have set up proper cleanup in your CI/CD pipeline
    DEPLOY_TO: 'production' # environment to deploy into - default is production – change this as needed, but be careful not to overwrite existing data on a shared volume due to concurrent writes (e.g., `staging` or similar names) and that you have set up proper cleanup in your CI/CD pipeline
    DEPLOYMENTS_STORE: /home/gitlab-runner # path where deployment files will go – change this as needed, but make sure it’s accessible from all nodes of your GitLab CI pipeline (e.g., samba share or network shared volume) and that no other job is using the same location
    DEPLOYMENTS_EXPIRATION: '30' # number of days for which deployment files should be stored – change this as needed, but make sure it’s a positive integer (e.g., `1` or `29`) and that you have set up proper cleanup in your CI/CD pipeline
    DEPLOY_TO: 'production' # environment to deploy into - default is production – change this as needed, but be careful not to overwrite existing data on a shared volume due to concurrent writes (e.g., `staging` or similar names) and that you have set up proper cleanup in your CI/CD pipeline
    DEPLOYMENTS_STORE: /home/gitlab-runner # path where deployment files will go – change this as needed, but make sure it’s accessible from all nodes of your GitLab CI pipeline (e.g., samba share or network shared volume) and that no other job is using the same location
    DEPLOYMENTS_EXPIRATION: '30' # number of days for which deployment files should be stored – change this as needed, but make sure it’s a positive integer (e.g., `1` or `29`) and that you have set up proper cleanup in your CI/CD pipeline
